evidence_quotes.quote: including micro transactions and gambling like mechanics in games where they are not even needed
gambling like mechanics in games where they are not even needed

paragraphs.text: Good day to you all! I'm writing this hoping it would help change something in the future. It's about the Stop Killing Video Games initiative, or Stop Destroying Games, depends on how you know it. Video Games are their own form of art, objectively good or bad. Doesn't matter. That's they way art is in general regardless of it's form. Each piece of art, has it's target audience and it's totally fine. As all other art forms have a way of being preserved and reused later at your own convenience, I strongly believe that digital goods that we have all paid for, must be protected and preserved. The Stop Destroying Games is not about having companies provide support for their games for the rest of eternity. It's about having an end of life plan that allows the users to still be able to enjoy the product they bought with their own money and good faith, without those companies having any way of being inconvenienced by it (no need for live service, internet connection for in-game products and more examples can come here). Therefore the users who acquired the product can enjoy it, and companies will not lose money off of it. A law against allowing companies end the service and completely destroy a piece of art, and history will be most beneficial, since some of the most capitalist and consumerism headed companies have their focus on making money, by releasing project after project and including micro transactions and gambling like mechanics in games where they are not even needed, rather than making a product that will last and provide an enjoyable experience to everyone. I strongly believe this will increase the quality of the products overall, there will be less focus on squeezing money out of people's pockets and more on how to create something that is worth buying. I sincerely hope you take this into consideration and find the best possible solution for having a way of enforcing companies to have and "End of Life plan" rather than end the product and move on to making more and siphoning more money. Thank you very much for reading this and have a great day all!
